factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: US Treasury yields rising sharply as inflation fears spike; Bloomberg reports 'Treasuries Lead Global Bond Yields Higher on Inflation Angst' with oil-driven inflation keeping rates elevated for longer. Higher real yields directly suppress gold demand.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY climbing above 99.00 for fifth consecutive day on strong retail sales data (+0.5% m/m) and Fed shift expectations. Stronger USD makes gold more expensive for foreign buyers and is single biggest headwind for XAUUSD.

factors.name: Inflation Data (Recent Surprises)
factors.summary: Japan PPI surged 4.9% y/y (expected +3.0%), well above consensus; energy shock from Iran war driving import prices +17.5% y/y. War-driven inflation reduces Fed rate-cut odds, pushing real yields higher and pressuring gold.

factors.name: Fed Policy Guidance
factors.summary: Fed Governor Barr stated 'I haven't decided on what to do at June FOMC meeting' but acknowledged energy inflation shock; NY Fed President Williams sees no case for rate moves and policy in 'good place.' Messaging leans hawkish given inflation, reducing safe-haven demand for gold.

factors.name: Oil Prices & Geopolitical Risk
factors.summary: WTI near $97.50, oil +1%+ on Trump-China deal and Trump's Iran warnings; Strait of Hormuz effectively closed. Elevated energy inflation is bearish for real yields (bullish gold normally), BUT it's also reducing rate-cut odds (bearish gold). Mixed net effect.

factors.name: Trump-Xi Summit & Trade Optimism
factors.summary: US-China trade truce extended, China committing double-digit billions in ag/oil purchases, 200 Boeing jets ordered. Risk-on sentiment reduces safe-haven demand for gold; equity futures initially rallied (then retreated on Iran rhetoric).

factors.name: Equity Market Sentiment
factors.summary: Global stocks initially surged (Korea KOSPI hit 8,046) then reversed 2%+ on Trump's Iran impatience warnings. Chop but net risk-off at close; gold not benefiting as safe-haven given DXY strength dominates.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: No fresh news on PBoC/RBI/Turkey gold purchases in this window. Structural bid is constant but not a tactical driver over 24-72h horizon.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations (Breakevens)
factors.summary: 5y5y forward inflation expectations rising due to energy shock; Michigan near-term inflation expectations elevated. But longer-term expectations (Fed Williams) described as 'stable.' Rate market is pricing less-dovish Fed, negative for gold.

factors.name: Price Action Context
factors.summary: XAUUSD down -0.37% in 4h and -1.93% in 24h. Move already underway in SELL direction; not extended yet but momentum is down and building.

screening.narrative: Gold is under structural pressure from a perfect storm of DXY strength (99.10+, five-day rally), rising real yields (energy-shock inflation pushing Treasuries higher despite no near-term Fed move), and reduced safe-haven demand (Trump-Xi trade optimism, equity recover attempts). Japan PPI surprise (4.9% vs 3.0% expected) is fueling global yield spike. Price action shows a -1.93% 24h selloff already in motion—this is Phase 2 (move building, not extended yet). The only offsetting factor is oil/geopolitical risk (Iran tensions, Hormuz closure), but that's being priced as inflation headwind (bearish for real yields, bearish for gold) rather than flight-to-safety.
catalysts_ahead: US CPI/Core CPI release (date TBD in next 2 weeks) — hot print would accelerate yields higher, bearish gold
FOMC minutes or Powell speech — any hawkish signal would reinforce DXY strength
BoJ rate decision (June likely) — any hike would steepen USD/JPY uptrend, bearish gold via yen carry unwind
Fed Barr/Williams further remarks — watching for pivot on inflation vs. employment trade-off
Trump-Iran escalation risk — could flip to flight-to-safety bid if headlines worsen
China PBOC any policy shift — yuan weakness (6.8415 fix today) suggests continued PBOC caution, supports USD strength
screening.risk_warning: Immediate invalidation: (1) Sharp geopolitical escalation or military action in Middle East (Hormuz closure, Iran-Israel direct conflict) would trigger safe-haven bid, overriding DXY strength. (2) Unexpected dovish Fed pivot (Barr/Powell dove signals) would spike real yields downward, bullish gold. (3) US equity crash would trigger flight-to-safety, gold spikes regardless of DXY. (4) A surprise cool CPI print in next 2 weeks would crater yields and reverse this SELL bias. Watch 24h price action — if XAUUSD bounces >0.8% off these levels, reassess Phase 2 pullback scenario.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (down -1.93% 24h, momentum building). Do NOT chase short here; wait for retest of $4,650–$4,680 resistance (intraday highs) for a cleaner short entry, or wait for pullback failure at support ($4,600) before adding. If geopolitical headlines flip, exit immediately. Confidence capped at 6 because major catalyst (FOMC, CPI) could flip the bias sharply higher in 1-2 weeks.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY climbs above 99.00 and on fifth consecutive day of gains (as of 02:20 UTC). Strong retail sales data (0.5% MoM in April) and Fed rate-hike expectations lifting USD across majors.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No explicit TIPS yield data in newsfeed; however, nominal 10y Treasury yields rising amid inflation concerns (oil-driven), which typically tightens the spread. Real yields likely flat to slightly firmer, mild headwind for gold.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran/Middle East)
factors.summary: Trump signals diminishing patience with Iran; oil rallies to $98.20–$98.50 WTI (two consecutive days of gains). Elevated energy costs and inflation fears drive risk-off and USD bid, temporarily competing with safe-haven gold demand.

factors.name: Oil Prices (WTI/Brent)
factors.summary: WTI rising ~$98.20, up for two straight days on Iran tensions and Trump-Xi summit dynamics. Higher oil feeds inflation expectations, which *can* be gold-positive long-term but near-term stokes Fed rate-hike bets, USD strength, and equity weakness—mixed for gold.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment / Equity Action
factors.summary: South Korean KOSPI reversed from record 8,046 to fall 2%+; Nasdaq futures down ~1.2%; EM stocks tumbling. Risk-off mood favors USD over gold; equities down suggests some flight-to-safety, but USD is outbidding gold as the safe-haven.

factors.name: Fed Policy Expectations
factors.summary: No new FOMC decision in window, but Fed's Barr (00:23 UTC) signals openness to a June rate hike; strong retail sales data (0.5% MoM) reinforces inflation pressure and Fed tightening expectations. Higher for longer rates weigh on gold.

factors.name: Trump-Xi Trade Summit
factors.summary: Trump claims 'fantastic trade deals' and China to buy US oil, ag goods, Boeing jets (200 units, vs. >500 hoped). Deal details modest; markets initially optimistic but pivoted to caution on Iran escalation risk—net neutral for gold, but USD strength beneficiary.

factors.name: Gold Price Action (Recent)
factors.summary: Gold has drifted below $4,600 and is on fourth straight down day per FXStreet (05:16 UTC). Pullback from monthly peak; momentum bearish and extended down move in progress.

factors.name: Inflation (Actual Data)
factors.summary: Japan wholesale prices surge 4.9% YoY (vs. 3.0% forecast) on Iran war-driven oil import spike; India raises fuel prices for first time in 4 years. Inflation surprise elevates real rates and feeds Fed tightening expectations, headwind for gold.

factors.name: Central Bank Flows / Structural Demand
factors.summary: No major CB gold buying or ETF flow data in window. Deutsche Bank scenario note gold could reach $8,000 in 5 years (long-term bullish), but no immediate structural bid evident.

screening.narrative: Gold has rolled over from monthly highs and fallen through $4,600 on its fourth consecutive down day, driven by a sustained USD rally (DXY +5 days, now >99.00) and firmer Fed tightening expectations sparked by strong US retail sales and Iran-linked oil inflation surprises. Risk-off sentiment in equities (KOSPI reversal, Nasdaq futures down 1.2%) is paradoxically favoring USD over traditional safe-haven gold, as the dollar benefits from rate-hike bets and geopolitical caution. The Trump-Xi summit was a modest anti-climax—trade concessions underwhelmed—and markets have refocused on Middle East tensions and energy cost shocks, which lift real yields expectations and compress gold's real return advantage.
catalysts_ahead: US PCE inflation print (if due in next 24–72h; would heavily influence gold direction)
FOMC meeting or Powell speech (no immediate date confirmed in window, but Fed Barr's June hike signal is live)
Oil/geopolitical escalation (Strait of Hormuz closure, Iran response to US-Israel strikes)
Bank of Japan June rate hike decision (Reuters poll: 65% expect 1.0% by June; BoJ yield curve policy decisions weigh on USD/JPY carry and gold)
US Treasury auction demand / yields (10y yields rising; key technical test)
screening.risk_warning: A surprise dovish Fed pivot, or BoJ delay on rate hikes, would crimp USD and real yields, reversing the SELL bias and sending gold back above $4,650–$4,700. Also, any major Middle East ceasefire announcement or Hormuz reopening would collapse oil prices and potentially crash inflation expectations, lifting gold quickly. Rapid reversal in equity sentiment back to risk-on could also redirect flows from USD into equities and gold.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (pullback underway 4 days, not yet extremely extended). Consider short entries on any intra-day bounce to $4,610–$4,620 resistance with tight stop above $4,635. Avoid chasing the downside; prefer to wait for a retest of $4,580–$4,590 support before committing to lower targets ($4,550). High-impact inflation or Fed data in next 48h could flip bias—reduce size and avoid overnight holds into PCE/FOMC clarity.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY) strength
factors.summary: USD extending rally on hawkish Fed repricing following strong US inflation and Retail Sales data. Multiple sources confirm dollar heading for best week since March (FXStreet, Bloomberg). This is the dominant near-term headwind for gold.

factors.name: Real yields (10y nominal + breakevens)
factors.summary: US Treasury yields jumping across the curve with 10-year near one-year highs and 30-year breaking above 5% threshold. Rising nominal yields + stable/rising breakevens = rising real yields, directly bearish for gold. No TIPS data in feed but curve action is unambiguously hawkish.

factors.name: Fed policy expectations (repricing)
factors.summary: Markets repricing in hawkish Fed expectations after strong inflation and retail data. Multiple sources cite 'rising expectations of hawkish Federal Reserve' (FXStreet). This is a fresh repricing this week, reversing prior dovish bets.

factors.name: Geopolitical risk (Middle East / Hormuz)
factors.summary: Trump-Xi summit ended with no substantive Iran breakthrough on Strait of Hormuz blockade. Oil prices remain elevated on supply concerns and Iranian tensions, but consensus appears to be status quo rather than escalation. Mixed short-term impact.

factors.name: Oil prices (Brent/WTI)
factors.summary: WTI breaking above $100 on Trump-China oil deal narrative, but this is inflationary input that supports Fed hawkishness rather than gold safe-haven bid. Higher oil = higher inflation expectations = higher real yields = bearish gold.

factors.name: Precious metals co-movement (silver weakness)
factors.summary: Silver (XAG/USD) crashed 6.02% from $83.47 to $78.44, now trading below $80. This cross-commodity weakness reflects broad deleveraging and risk-off, with silver breaking below key technical support. Suggests gold weakness is structural, not just technical.

factors.name: Equity/risk sentiment
factors.summary: Nasdaq futures down ~1.2%, S&P 500 down 0.56%, Dow down 0.35% as markets enter 'sell-the-news' phase after Trump-Xi summit. Risk-off wave sweeping across, but equities still near record highs — not yet a panic/safe-haven trigger for gold.

factors.name: EUR/USD weakness
factors.summary: EUR/USD at fresh 5-week lows near 1.1630 as wider UST/Bund spreads widen and USD strengthens. Euro broken below 50/200-day MA. Stronger dollar is inversely correlated to gold — bullish for DXY, bearish for XAU/USD.

factors.name: Price action momentum (4h/24h)
factors.summary: Gold down 0.51% in 4h and -1.27% in 24h, falling for fourth straight day near $4,550. Move is already underway; no fresh catalyst in last few hours to suggest reversal imminent. Technical momentum is downward.

factors.name: Central bank policy (ECB, BoE, BoJ)
factors.summary: No major ECB/BoE/BoJ decisions in news window. BoJ intervention rumors persist but JPY remains weak despite MoF efforts. Dovish signals from Czech National Bank and Hungarian MNB are secondary. US Fed repricing dominates.

screening.narrative: Gold is in PHASE 3 of a bearish move triggered by a sharp repricing of Fed policy higher this week following strong US inflation and retail sales data. The US Dollar is rallying strongly (best week since March) as real yields rise across the curve, with 10-year Treasury yields near one-year highs and 30-year yields above 5%. Silver's crash of 6% reinforces broad deleveraging and confirms the weakness is structural rather than technical. While geopolitical tensions remain (Middle East, Hormuz strait) and oil prices are elevated, the dominant narrative is USD strength and rising rate expectations, both bearish for gold. The move is now extended (fourth consecutive down day, -1.27% in 24h), placing this late in a downtrend.
catalysts_ahead: US CPI/PCE data (if scheduled next week) — any reacceleration would confirm hawkish Fed repricing, extend gold weakness
Federal Reserve speakers (Powell, Williams, Waller) — any hawkish comments could extend USD rally, compress gold further
University of Michigan inflation expectations survey — rising expectations would lift real yields
Weekly jobless claims and NFP (if scheduled) — labor market strength supports Fed hawkishness
Middle East escalation or Strait of Hormuz closure — could trigger geopolitical flight-to-safety bid and reverse gold lower-bound
Bank of Japan intervention or yen carry unwind — sudden JPY strength could trigger risk-off and safe-haven gold bid
Trump tariff announcements post-China visit — could impact inflation expectations and Fed path
screening.risk_warning: Move is already extended (PHASE 3); risk is overshooting into real yields peak and USD consolidation trigger a pullback/reversal. If Middle East escalates (Hormuz closure, direct Iran-US confrontation) or geopolitical risk spikes sharply, gold could rebound as safe-haven despite higher rates. If Fed speakers turn dovish or CPI disappoints to the downside, rate expectations would reset and long gold could reverse. Carry trade unwind (JPY spike, risk-off) could also force short covering. Watch for technical bounce at $4,530–$4,500 and retest of recent resistance ($4,575) as potential reversal setup.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended and late in the downtrend (PHASE 3); avoid chasing shorts here. Consider small short entries on retest of $4,575 resistance or wait for pullback to $4,500–$4,530 support before re-engaging bears. Look for Fed speakers or CPI data to confirm higher rate trajectory before extending shorts further. Better risk/reward for longs on capitulation near $4,500 if geopolitical fears surface or technical support holds.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: US Treasury yields surging across the curve (10y near one-year high, 30y above 5%), and with sticky inflation data this week (PPI accelerating per news), real yields are rising materially. Higher real yields directly compress gold valuations.

factors.name: USD Index Strength
factors.summary: DXY rallying on hawkish Fed repricing expectations; strong USD data and oil prices lifting bond yields. EUR/USD broke below 1.170 to lowest in over a month (~1.163). Stronger dollar is directly bearish for gold.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy Repricing
factors.summary: Markets are pricing in Fed rate hike expectations over the next year following strong US inflation and retail sales data. New Fed chair Kevin Warsh is being tested by the bond selloff; market is hawkish, not dovish.

factors.name: Inflation Data / Expectations
factors.summary: PPI accelerating (per Bloomberg 10:43), producer costs up, and oil prices at elevated levels ($100+ WTI). Inflation expectations rising, not falling. This pressures real yields and shifts Fed pricing hawkish, both bearish for gold.

factors.name: Oil Prices / Geopolitical Premium
factors.summary: WTI broke above $100 on Trump-China summit and Iranian tensions, but this inflation input is BEARISH for gold because it forces higher real rates. Oil-driven inflation = Fed tightening = lower gold.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment / Equities
factors.summary: Dow Jones futures down 0.35%, S&P 500 futures down 0.56%, Nasdaq down 0.81%. Equities under pressure on profit-taking and inflation fears. Gold typically benefits in risk-off, but nominal yield gains dominate; equities weakening suggests no safe-haven bid yet.

factors.name: Trump-Xi Summit Outcome
factors.summary: Summit concluded with business-first tone but no major trade breakthroughs; China agrees to buy Boeing and US oil, but Iran/Taiwan issues unresolved. Geopolitical risk remains elevated but no major shock pricing in yet.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows & Physical Demand
factors.summary: No recent ETF flow data in news. India gold imports slowing due to rupee weakness and new trade restrictions (per Bloomberg 09:37); seasonal tailwinds muted by macro headwinds.

factors.name: Price Action Confirmation
factors.summary: Gold down 1.16% in 24h and 0.05% in 4h; falling for fourth straight day near $4,550 support. Move is extended and sustained in bearish direction, NOT fresh.

factors.name: Bias History Consistency
factors.summary: Prior three reads (03:37, 06:54, 09:47) all SELL 6/10 with no confirmation triggered. This read maintains the SELL but now higher conviction due to hawkish repricing catalyst and move extension.

key_levels.support: $4,550 (current test area, weekly low); $4,500 (round number); $4,480 (recent consolidation low)
key_levels.resistance: $4,575–$4,600 (intraday and weekly highs before breakdown); $4,620 (prior swing high)

screening.narrative: Gold is in a sustained bearish phase as the bond market reprices Fed policy sharply hawkish on the back of sticky inflation data (PPI acceleration, strong retail sales, oil spike). US 10-year yields hit near one-year highs, 30-year yields broke 5%, and real yields are rising—the single most correlated negative factor for gold. The USD is rallying hard (EUR/USD below 1.170, DXY strengthening), which compounds the headwind. Gold has now fallen four consecutive days and is testing key support near $4,550. The Trump-Xi summit provided no major relief and underscored geopolitical risk premium in oil (+$100 WTI), but oil-driven inflation is pushing rates *higher*, not lower—bearish for precious metals.
catalysts_ahead: PCE release (if scheduled early next week) — will confirm/refute inflation trajectory; if hot, further USD/yield rally
Fed speakers (Barr already flagged inflation risks; Warsh testing markets) — any hawkish commentary extends bear case
Treasury auction schedule and 2s10s curve inversion watch
Crude oil supply updates (Strait of Hormuz, OPEC, Iran developments) — geopolitical tail risk but macro-dependent
China economic data (manufacturing PMI, factory output) — could shift risk sentiment
screening.risk_warning: If US inflation surprise comes in COOLER than expected (CPI/PCE miss to downside), real yields could reverse sharply lower, triggering an immediate short-cover rally in gold back above $4,600. Additionally, a sudden risk-off equity cascade (stock market crash) or geopolitical escalation (Iran strikes directly, Taiwan tensions spike) could flip gold to safe-haven bid despite elevated yields. Finally, if Fed speakers turn dovish (acknowledging yield damage or recession risk), gold reversal is possible.
screening.entry_guidance: Move already extended down 4 days; avoid chasing shorts. Wait for a retest of $4,575–$4,600 resistance (pullback or intraday bounce) before fresh short entry. If break below $4,550 support on high volume, watch $4,500 as next target, but be ready for mean reversion into end-of-week/weekend profit-taking if equities stabilize.
